programmes.show_description: A collection of Elaine Paige's personal favourites from the BBC's song and dance archives, including songs from Cats, Sunset Boulevard, My Fair Lady, West Side Story and The Sound of Music.
BBC archive performances from musicals, including Ella Fitzgerald singing Mack the Knife, Captain Sensible performing a classic from South Pacific and Jay Z taking on Annie.
3/6. Carey searches for one suspect, whilst Frank Napier deals with another. Contains strong language, graphic violence and upsetting scenes.
Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programmes.show_name: The A to Z of TV Gardening
programmes.show_description: 1/25. A look at some of the BBC's most popular gardening programmes and personalities, presented by Carol Kirkwood. Carol explores subjects that all begin with the letter A.
11/15. We're in the stunning Highlands and Islands of Scotland. First to go is army cadet trainer Daisy, who plans to march the recruits up one of Scotland's most iconic mountains.
Countryfile explores life after dark on the North York Moors, one of the UK's Dark Sky Reserves.
2/2. Ian Hamilton and his guide dog Major head to one of the most westerly towns in the UK - Campbeltown, in the stunning Mull of Kintyre.

programmes.show_name: My Kind of Town: Blairgowrie and Rattray
programmes.show_description: 1/2. Ian Hamilton and his guide dog Major are off to Blairgowrie and Rattray in Perthshire.
A feast of food from Niklas Ekstedt, Vivek Singh, Shivi Ramoutar and Big Zuu. Also, Rick Stein and Clive Myrie head out of the studio and across the globe.
14/29. The remaining three chefs from north east England and Yorkshire compete over mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. Who will win, and who must go home?
15/29. Only one chef can go through to represent north east England and Yorkshire.
6/6. For Mary Berry, watching the pennies doesn't mean sacrificing flavour. With her secrets of cooking from scratch in a frugal kitchen, even an economical steak is on the menu.
Highlights from the 2026 World Figure Skating Championships in Prague.
Agatha Christie's murder mystery. Detective Hercule Poirot sets out to discover the truth behind the death of a stage star at a resort hotel on an Adriatic island. [1982]
4/6. The reunited Xudum pride mums struggle to feed so many mouths, and one cub is close to starving. Lediba and Pobe battle to keep their cubs safe from intruding male leopards.
5/12. Callum and Zara believe their newborn lambs are vulnerable to bird attacks, Lucy and Adam face selling their herd if the new farm falls through, and Emma prepares for an open farm day.
1/2. Simon travels to Burma and Bangladesh to learn about the roots and consequences of the Rohingya refugee crisis.
Police race to identify an intruder after a child is sexually assaulted in her bedroom. Contains scenes which some viewers may find disturbing.

programmes.show_name: Master and Commander: The Far Side of the World
programmes.show_description: 1805. Chasing an enemy ship, Jack Aubrey demands much of his crew - perhaps too much. Contains some strong language. [2003]
A music lover decides to open a record shop in 1970s Belfast and call it Good Vibrations. Contains very strong language and some violent scenes. [2013]
Fiona Bruce presents an hour of topical debate from Clacton-on-Sea. On the panel are Jake Richards, Tom Tugendhat, Layla Moran and Tom Skinner.

programmes.show_name: Clash of the Superpowers: America vs China
programmes.show_description: 1/2. In his first term, Trump upends America's relationship with Beijing.
Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.
4/12. Clive Myrie encourages distillery manager Luke in Bristol to replace coffee and energy drinks with black tea to keep stress at bay.

programmes.show_name: The Repair Shop on the Road
programmes.show_description: The team restores a bowling club sign in Scotland's smallest county and a Victorian clock with haunted Hebridean roots. Meanwhile, Will learns coppersmithing for whisky stills.
It's sisters versus friends in this episode from Somerset. In the saleroom, both experts surprise their teams with silver - but which bonus buys will the bidders take a shine to?
Tim tackles a battered unicycle, Mark fixes an iconic 80s gadget, Brenton takes on an Edwardian plate camera, and David repairs a model of a virus that changed medical history.
Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.
26/30. Game show in which players score points by answering questions correctly while avoiding the Impossible answers.
11/25. In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches. Hosted by Alex Scott.
1/25. Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz where six plucky contestants must stay out of the light to be alright. Can anyone beat the lightning and win the £3,000 jackpot?
9/15. Clive submerges himself in the famous hot springs of Saturnia and joins Italy's last surviving cowboys, known as the Butteri, to learn how to lasso and barbecue the perfect steak.
3/3. A tiny chameleon faces the journey of a lifetime in a Madagascan forest, and a water vole in Scotland swims across a loch and scales a mountain to find new territory and a mate.
15/26. Michael Aspel and a team of experts examine curios and artefacts offered up by the public. This edition comes from Lochgilphead in Argyll and Bute.
29/40. In Blackburn, Anita Manning discovers an Omar Ramsden spoon.
1/100. Ahir Shah, Michaela Strachan, Dr Xand van Tulleken and Vogue Williams test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.
2/15. In Argyll, Rick meets a Michelin Green Star-winning chef who cooks local and foraged ingredients. He catches langoustine with a teacher-turned-fisherwoman and makes tattie scones.

1/6. Chris 'Flamebaster' Roberts dives into Cardigan's wild west: oracle fishing, driftwood crab feasts and wood-fired calzones bursting with local flavour.
28/31. Semi-final subjects include George Frideric Handel and the films of Christopher Nolan.
34/37. It's the last quarter-final, and only one place remains in the semi-finals - who will take it? Amol Rajan asks the questions.

programmes.show_name: Clash of the Superpowers: America vs China
programmes.show_description: 2/2. As the battle for technological supremacy ramps up, a controversial visit to Taiwan brings the two superpowers closer to a dangerous confrontation.
2/8. Bob and Paul travel overseas for an all-out Irish fishing trip - it's their first time fishing the rivers of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland.
Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Patrick O'Connell.   Followed by Weather.
